basics.description: Deploy a Keysight CloudLens Virtual Packet Broker (vPB) v3.15.0 from Azure Marketplace. vPB aggregates, filters, and forwards mirrored network traffic to monitoring tools. After deployment, SSH is reachable in about 60 seconds and full vPB initialization completes in 5 to 10 minutes. Default CLI credentials: admin / ixia (change immediately).

location.label: Region
location.toolTip: Azure region for deployment. Pick the region that hosts the workloads you want to mirror.

elements.name: vmName
elements.label: VM name
elements.toolTip: Name of the vPB VM. Used as the computer name and as a prefix for the public IP, NSG, the three NICs, and (when creating one) the VNet.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 3 to 64 characters. Lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only. Must start and end with a letter or digit.

elements.name: adminUsername
elements.label: Linux admin username
elements.toolTip: OS-level admin user for SSH on port 9022 (KCOS images do NOT use port 22). The vPB CLI uses its own separate admin account (admin / ixia) reachable via two-hop SSH on port 2222 from inside the OS shell.

options.text: Heads-up: the password below is for **OS-level access** on the underlying VM (only used for low-level troubleshooting). The vPB **CLI** runs inside a Kubernetes pod and uses a separate set of credentials configured during initialization. See the OPERATIONS doc.

toolTip.password: OS-level SSH password. 12+ chars with uppercase, lowercase, a digit, and a symbol. NOT the web UI password.

elements.name: vmSize
elements.label: VM size
elements.toolTip: Minimum Standard_D8s_v3 required: vPB needs three NICs with accelerated networking, and smaller D-series sizes do not support 3+ NICs.

options.text: **NIC layout:** vPB always has 1 management NIC. By default it also has 1 ingress NIC (receives mirror traffic) and 1 egress NIC (forwards to tools). For production fan-in/fan-out scenarios you can add up to 3 of each. **Azure NIC limits per VM size:** D8s_v3 supports 4 NICs (use ingress+egress <= 3), D16s_v3 / D32s_v3 support 8 NICs (use ingress+egress <= 7).

elements.name: ingressNicCount
elements.label: Ingress NIC count
elements.toolTip: Number of ingress NICs (receive mirror traffic from sensors / Gateway Load Balancer). Each extra NIC counts toward the VM's NIC quota.

elements.name: egressNicCount
elements.label: Egress NIC count
elements.toolTip: Number of egress NICs (forward processed traffic to monitoring tools). Each extra NIC counts toward the VM's NIC quota.

elements.name: vnetMode
elements.label: Virtual network
elements.toolTip: Create a new VNet with three subnets (mgmt, ingress, egress), or attach to a VNet you already own that already has those three subnets.

elements.name: addressSpace
elements.label: VNet address space (CIDR)
elements.toolTip: Address space for the new VNet. Must not overlap with VNets you plan to peer with.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid CIDR block (for example, 10.50.0.0/16).

elements.name: mgmtSubnetPrefix
elements.label: Management subnet prefix (CIDR)
elements.toolTip: Hosts the management NIC (public IP + SSH access).

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid CIDR block (for example, 10.50.2.0/24).

elements.name: ingressSubnetPrefix
elements.label: Ingress subnet prefix (CIDR)
elements.toolTip: Receives mirrored traffic from sensors and Azure Gateway Load Balancer.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid CIDR block (for example, 10.50.3.0/24).

elements.name: egressSubnetPrefix
elements.label: Egress subnet prefix (CIDR)
elements.toolTip: Forwards processed mirror traffic to your monitoring tools.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid CIDR block (for example, 10.50.4.0/24).

elements.name: existingVnetName
elements.label: Existing VNet name
elements.toolTip: Name of the existing VNet. It must already contain subnets named vpb-mgmt, vpb-ingress, and vpb-egress.

elements.name: existingVnetResourceGroup
elements.label: Existing VNet resource group
elements.toolTip: Resource group of the existing VNet. Leave blank to use this deployment's resource group.
